title: Structured Methods Library — method-composition page across Heuer, structured analytic techniques, and intelligence methodology

fields.vision: A general analytical-methodology library: the place where method families, source authorities, and reusable tradecraft moves are catalogued and made checkable.
fields.mechanism: Four mappings define every method in the catalogue: the question the method answers, the canonical source it derives from, the analytical trap it guards against, and the way the method composes with other methods.
fields.built_components: The aggregation page renders with the three mapping lenses (question / source / trap) in place. The method-composition page is partially built and continues to grow as new method combinations are exercised in the rest of the portfolio.
fields.source_utilization: Source use is organized by role: method authorities, domain evidence, technical background, implementation evidence, and claim limits. The page should show why each source matters; direct quotations and stronger factual claims still require exact passage anchors before publication.
fields.plan: Continue building out the method-composition page as new combinations are exercised across the portfolio. Bind each method's canonical source through the source-register pipeline so the method-to-source binding is checkable rather than asserted. Step 01 Method-composition build-out New combinations of methods exercised across the portfolio added to the composition page as they appear. Step 02 Canonical-source binding Each method's canonical source bound through the source-register pipeline. Step 03 Checkable method-to-source Method-to-source binding becomes checkable rather than asserted, on the same standard as the bias atlas and the structured analytic techniques toolkit.
fields.status: Status: The aggregation page renders with the three mapping lenses (question / source / trap) in place. The method-composition page is partially built and continues to grow as new method combinations are exercised in the rest of the portfolio.
fields.remaining_gaps: Remaining gaps: continue building out the method-composition page as new combinations are exercised across the portfolio. Bind each method's canonical source through the source-register pipeline so the method-to-source binding is checkable rather than asserted.
fields.detailed_source_vision: A general analytical-methodology library: the place where method families, source authorities, and reusable tradecraft moves are catalogued and made checkable. It is distinct from the Structured Techniques Toolkit. The library explains what a method is for, what canonical literature supports it, and what analytical trap it guards against; the toolkit turns selected methods into usable instruments.
fields.detailed_source_mechanism: Four mappings define every method in the catalogue: the question the method answers, the canonical source it derives from, the analytical trap it guards against, and the way the method composes with other methods.

maintenance_rule: Update this blueprint before strengthening public claims on the website. Do not use word count, module count, or status percentage as a substitute for concrete project substance.
claim_ceiling_rule: Public claims may rise only when the built components, source use, status, and remaining gaps are updated here and reflected in the rendered page.

public_standard_check.Vision: This document supports the public account of Blueprint by preserving the project purpose, reader-facing claim, and professional-portfolio role.
public_standard_check.Mechanism: It connects source material, project components, implementation state, and next actions so the work can be inspected rather than merely asserted.
public_standard_check.What is built: The supporting evidence is the named page, blueprint, packet, project file, source record, or implementation page described in the document.
public_standard_check.Source use: Sources are used as method and source background, domain grounding, or project history; direct quotation and stronger factual claims require passage-level review.
public_standard_check.Plan: Bind claims to exact source anchors, visible implementation evidence, or a narrower public claim.
public_standard_check.Status: Current support record for the professional package unless explicitly marked archival.
public_standard_check.Remaining Gaps: Passage-level source check, implementation verification, citation cleanup, and proportional public wording remain open where evidence is incomplete.

public_pitch.what_it_is: A general analytical-methodology library: the place where method families, source authorities, and reusable tradecraft moves are catalogued and made checkable.
public_pitch.distinctive_mechanism: Four mappings define every method in the catalogue: the question the method answers, the canonical source it derives from, the analytical trap it guards against, and the way the method composes with other methods.
public_pitch.why_it_matters: It turns analytic methods into reusable instruments with inputs, steps, failure modes, interpretation rules, and reviewer actions.
public_pitch.pitch_integrity_note: Mechanism-first public pitch inserted after the Nexus Lab mechanism-erasure audit on 2026-05-13; source basis: blueprint detailed source fields.

pitch_integrity_audit.standard: object, distinctive mechanism, value, built state, and non-overclaim discipline must be visible.

researcher_process_map.summary: A general analytical-methodology library: the place where method families, source authorities, and reusable tradecraft moves are catalogued and made checkable.
researcher_process_map.final_use_case: It turns analytic methods into reusable instruments with inputs, steps, failure modes, interpretation rules, and reviewer actions.

instruments.input: intelligence or research question, decision context, evidence type, uncertainty level, and user role
instruments.operation: selects the structured analytic technique or technique sequence that fits the task rather than applying SAT labels ornamentally
instruments.output: method assignment and technique rationale as a reviewable artifact
instruments.upstream_dependency: Requires intelligence or research question, decision context, evidence type, uncertainty level, and user role
instruments.downstream_use: Feeds method assignment and technique rationale as a reviewable artifact; later claims should not rely on this step until its expected output is present.
instruments.proof: Expected proof artifact: method assignment and technique rationale as a reviewable artifact
instruments.status: Status: The aggregation page renders with the three mapping lenses (question / source / trap) in place. The method-composition page is partially built and.
instruments.gap: Verify one representative case: the step should accept intelligence or research question, decision context, evidence type, uncertainty level, and user role and produce method assignment and technique rationale as a.
instruments.verification_check: Run one analytic question through Task And Method Assignment; confirm evidence, assumptions, method decision, confidence limit, and human/AI delta.

instruments.input: source record, assumptions, alternatives, indicators, confidence basis, and possible deception conditions
instruments.operation: separates evidence from assumptions before the analytic method begins; the step records the decision path so the reader can see how the project moves from input to governed output.
instruments.output: evidence/assumption ledger as a reviewable artifact
